channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Healthy living, party unity, and 'time to smell the roses': Congressional Republicans' New Year's resolutions
item.description: House and Senate lawmakers spoke with Fox News Digital about what their New Year's resolutions are as Republicans prepare to take the levers of power in DC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he is planning to attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Tuesday that he's planning to attend the funeral of former President Jimmy Carter. Asked about it as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump responded, "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US prepares to deorbit International Space Station amid China competition
item.description: As competition in the new space race ramps up with China, the United States is preparing to deorbit the International Space Station. If a replacement is not ready by 2028, China may become the leader in space.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kevin Cramer’s son sentenced to 28 years over chase, fatal car crash
item.description: The son of North Dakota Sen. Kevin Cramer (R) was sentenced Monday to 28 years in prison over a police chase and death of a local sheriff’s deputy. Ian Cramer, 43, was charged with manslaughter last December after he crashed his family’s SUV into a police cruiser, concluding an hourlong chase.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Texas Supreme Court dismisses State Bar lawsuit against assistant attorney general
item.description: The state bar sought to take away the law license of Assistant Attorney General Brent Webster over a Texas lawsuit challenging 2020 presidential election results.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jess Huff, KPRC 2

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Imperial Beach mayor vying for San Diego County Supervisor
item.description: After San Diego County Board of Supervisors Chair Nora Vargas announced her decision to vacate her position at the end of her current term, three people have announced their desire to sit in her place.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Biden doesn't believe he should have dropped out of race: Source
item.description: President Joe Biden told aides privately that he believes he could have defeated Donald Trump had he remained in race
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: GOP mocks Gavin Newsom’s ‘brag’ over modest increase in homelessness hike
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom was ripped for his criticism of an analysis of California's continued increase in homelessness, as Republicans said no increase is celebratory.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Pentagon chief loses bid to reject 9/11 plea deals
item.description: A military appeals court has ruled against Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin's effort to throw out the plea deals reached for Khalid Sheikh Mohammed and two other defendants in the 9/11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The rise in mail voting comes with a price, as mismatched signatures lead to ballot rejections
item.description: The use of mail ballots exploded in 2020 as states looked for ways to accommodate voters during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy And Ayanna Alexander, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump tests influence over GOP with Johnson Speakership vote
item.description: The strength of President-elect Trump’s influence over the razor-thin House Republican majority will get another major test Friday, when Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) aims to be reelected as Speaker.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carter helped shape modern day border policies, former aide says
item.description: He described how Carter treated everyone with respect, but "not the kind of guy you could pull a fast one on, he was very smart and a very tough boss.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: DNC chair candidate calls for greater investment in GOP states
item.description: In the four-point platform that was first seen by The Hill, Skoufis vowed to “dramatically” enhance the party’s Red State Fund if elected chair.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Allman Brothers’ Chuck Leavell details Jimmy Carter’s musical legacy
item.description: "He was such a likable human being," said Chuck Leavell, former Allman Brothers Band member and current Rolling Stones touring keyboardist.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A 34-year-old soldier serving with the Indiana National Guard in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident, the Army said Monday. Capt. Eric Richard Hart, 34, of Indianapolis, Indiana, died Saturday in Iraq, the Army National Guard said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is being mourned in his tiny hometown and around the world
item.description: Tributes to former President Jimmy Carter are pouring in from his small hometown of Plains, Georgia, and around the world.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Sharon Johnson And Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden closes government as 'mark of respect' for late former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Monday signed an executive order closing executive offices of the government for late former President Jimmy Carter's funeral on Jan. 9.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump's Vote of Confidence
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces nearly $2.5 billion more in military aid for Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that the United States will send nearly $2.5 billion more in weapons to Ukraine as his administration works quickly to spend all the money it has available to help Kyiv fight off Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An appeals court upholds a $5 million award in a sexual abuse verdict against President-elect Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal appeals court on Monday upheld a jury’s finding in a civil case that Donald Trump sexually abused a columnist in an upscale department store dressing room in the mid-1990s. The 2nd U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump endorses Mike Johnson to stay on as House Speaker despite government funding turmoil
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday endorsed House Speaker Mike Johnson, providing crucial backing for the Louisiana Republican as he prepares for what is expected to be another contentious speakership race this week.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A caucus of military veterans seeks to bridge the political divide in a polarized Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The players in this year's Army-Navy football game kept up a long tradition, with each side honoring the other’s school song when the contest ended. It was an acknowledgment that the future could bring moments when the opponents that day are teammates on deadlier fields.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How American presidents have planned their own funerals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter's memorial journey will end at his house in the tiny town of Plains, Georgia, where he grew up on a peanut farm. That is where his wife, Rosalynn, was laid to rest last year in a burial plot that they chose years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is an example of ‘simple decency': Joe Biden
item.description: President Biden said former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday, “lived a life measured not by words but by deeds.
item.dc:creator: Brian Howard, NewsNation

item.title: Political world offers condolences for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Members of the political world offered condolences and warm wishes for the family of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday afternoon. They honored Carter’s decades of achievement and service to the country. Carter, who turned 100 earlier this year, died peacefully in Palins, Ga.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Jimmy Carter's marriage to Rosalynn Carter helped shape his legacy
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter’s marriage to first lady Rosalynn Carter played a defining role in his life, spanning 77 years and making it the longest presidential marriage in U.S. history. Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter 'was most impactful one-term president': Stuart Eizenstat
item.description: He also credited Carter for initiating defense programs that would later be associated with the Reagan administration.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California and national leaders honor former President Jimmy Carter after his passing
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — On Dec. 29, Jimmy Carter, the 39th president of the United States and a 2002 Nobel Peace Prize winner, died in his home in Plains, Georgia, according to the Carter Center. He was the longest-lived president at 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: Adoreil Ayoubgoulan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politicians mourn Jimmy Carter's death
item.description: “Today, America and the world lost an extraordinary leader, statesman, and humanitarian,” President Joe Biden said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Carter to be honored with events in Georgia, Washington
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter will be honored with public observances in both Georgia and Washington, D.C., in the coming days, following his death on Sunday. He will be interred in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, NewsNation

item.title: In Photos: Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center. Here's a look at his life and times in photos. He had turned 100 in October, making him the longest-lived president in the nation’s history.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Another Florida state representative switches from Democrat to Republican
item.description: For the second time this year, a Florida Democratic lawmaker has switched their party affiliation after winning their seat.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bald eagle finally becomes official US bird
item.description: The eagle had long been associated with the U.S., but it only just officially became the national bird.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
